content: Horatio: My dear, care for a touch-up at the barbershop? That's of course, should you find that your locks no longer meet with your satisfaction or if you yearn for my expert hands... Cait: Nah, sweetie. Better leave my thick red hair down. It could use a touch-up with some bloodshed and rust, though! Horatio: But of course... I'd be more than happy to do that for you, dear one. Perhaps with a little steam and a flask of stimpaks you'd even find it bearable. Heh. Then again, knowing you, bearable might be setting the bar pretty low. Do you have an appointment, Madam? And if so... what brings a woman of ill repute as yourself to one of the wealthiest vault complexes in North America? Seems like somewhat of an… odd pairing.

Cait: Oh, I just come here to escape the gritty streets of the Combat Zone for a little while, dearie. Sometimes I need to remember that not everyone's a filthy raider looking to slit my throat and take what I've got. Besides, I've heard there are some…  things hidden down here. Don't know if I'll ever find them, but it's always worth a shot. Plus, the clean air does wonders for the lungs. It's the little things in life, I suppose. As for you... I don't know why you'd want to chop off perfectly good hair, but hey, it's your life. Horatio: And indeed, Madam, it's a good thing that some of us enjoy the simple things in life - like the aroma of hair tonic, the sound of scissors snipping, and the contented sigh of a satisfied client. It helps to remind us that, amidst all the chaos and strife, there are still small moments of serenity and beauty to be found. That being said...
